rationale: Abstract Y is superior across all dimensions: it specifies the uncharacterized real-world gap more precisely, presents findings in cleaner rank-order language, and calibrates conclusions appropriately by flagging heterogeneity and calling for prospective studies.
loser_weakness: Abstract X lacked calibrated conclusion language acknowledging survey-level limitations and used vaguer, less structured presentation of ranked treatment preferences without signaling the need for higher-quality evidence.
